title: Parallelize V3FuncOpt using V3ThreadScope
body:
## Summary

Apply the same per-function parallel processing pattern used in V3VariableOrder and V3EmitCImp to V3FuncOpt. Each AstCFunc is now processed independently in parallel using V3ThreadScope, improving compilation speed on multi-module designs.

### Changes
- Add V3ThreadScope to funcOptAll() to parallelize function processing
- Convert FuncOptStats to use std::atomic<uint64_t> for thread-safe updates
- Add VL_MT_SAFE annotation to FuncOptVisitor::apply()

### Thread Safety Analysis

| Component | Thread-Safe? | Notes |
|-----------|--------------|-------|
| AST traversal per-function | Yes | Each function's subtree is disjoint |
| user1() usage | Yes | Only marks nodes within the function being processed |
| FuncOptStats | Yes | Uses atomic counters |
| VNUser1InUse | Yes | Claimed before parallel section starts |

## Test plan
- Existing regression tests pass
- CI build completes successfully
- Verify identical output with --threads 1 vs --threads N

Generated with Claude Code
